fixture.source_domain: SQL preparation, placeholders, identifier quoting, and escaping

cases.focus: null query returns null without touching placeholders
%i/%d/%s/%f placeholder parsing, identifier quoting, string quoting, and float normalization
single array argument is unpacked like vsprintf()
literal percent escaping and placeholder escape insertion
numbered placeholders, padding, precision, and backward-compatible unquoted numbered strings
same argument cannot be both identifier and value
too few and too many arguments preserve WordPress return contracts
non-scalar values trigger doing_it_wrong and preserve old empty-string escaping behavior
allow_unsafe_unquoted_parameters changes legacy percent/string quoting behavior
LIKE wildcard escaping and backtick identifier doubling
global esc_sql facade delegates to wpdb::_escape for scalar and nested array values
by-reference scalar mutation and float no-op behavior
documented no-connection scalar and non-scalar fallback behavior
placeholder token insertion, removal, and query filter registration

native_boundaries.reason: wpdb::_real_escape() normally delegates to mysqli_real_escape_string() when a live database handle exists. This fixture records the deterministic no-connection fallback; live mysqli escaping remains a later WPHX-305 database execution gate.
wpdb::placeholder_escape() registers remove_placeholder_escape() on the native WordPress query filter. The fixture exercises this registration through the mirrored plugin API.

Invalid placeholder and unsupported value cases call wp_load_translations_early(), __(), _doing_it_wrong(), and the doing_it_wrong_run hook. The probe stubs translation output but records diagnostics and hook emission.
wpdb::escape_by_ref() mutates non-float values by PHP reference; Haxe candidates must preserve the public reference contract at the generated PHP boundary.

remaining_gaps.detail: The candidate still subclasses upstream wpdb for untouched behavior. A no-fallback packaged-distribution gate is tracked separately once enough method bodies are Haxe-owned.
This candidate preserves the deterministic no-connection escaping cases. Full mysqli_real_escape_string() parity belongs with live database execution gates.
The fixture verifies query-filter registration and remove_placeholder_escape() round trips, but full wpdb::query() filter execution belongs with query/result fixtures.
This fixture covers seed traces from the upstream db tests. Full upstream wpdb PHPUnit parity remains a domain-level closure requirement.
